message:
perf(mvcc): swap HandleView page sets from HashSet to sorted Vec

`HandleView::new` was the residual SSI commit hot path called out in
docs/perf-mvcc-session-20260424.md (0.10% MT8 self-time on
profiling-mt-mvcc-20260424T161631Z). The constructor built six
`HashSet<PageNumber>`/`HashSet<(PageNumber, u64)>` fields with the
default `RandomState`, paying SipHash13 on every insert and again on
every overlap probe in `check_read_overlap` / `check_write_overlap`.

`summarize_witness_keys` already returns `Vec<PageNumber>`s sorted and
dedup'd; the old code re-collected them into a HashSet — pure waste.
Switch the six fields to sorted `Vec` and query via `binary_search`.
The four page-summary fields move in directly without a re-sort; the
two `(PageNumber, u64)` exact-cell fields drop `leaf_page` from the
sorted full-key tuple (so dups are possible across distinct leaves)
and need a single sort+dedup. `write_pages` also continues to need one
sort+dedup because it unions the unsorted
`tracked_write_conflict_pages_iter` with the witness-summary pages.

Microbench (`bench_handle_view_new_typical`, 10 read-witness pages +
10 staged write pages, 200K builds × 7 trials):
  baseline (HashSet, default RandomState): median 1674.8 ns/build
  optimised (sorted Vec, binary_search):   median  970.8 ns/build
  delta: -42.0% (~1.7x faster)

Correctness: 1344 pass / 7 fail, identical to the pre-existing
concurrent-agent failure set listed in the prior cc_4 session memory
(begin_concurrent::test_zero_witness_commit_stays_candidate_free…,
core_types::test_cleanup_orphaned_slots…,
history_compression::test_page_history_newest_full_image…,
lifecycle::{test_concurrent_same_page_writers…, test_publish_write_set…,
test_write_skew_sum_constraint…}, shared_lock_table::test_try_acquire_conflict…).
The seqlock writer-blocks-readers test flaked in the parallel run and
passed on rerun.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
